_meta.comment: Single source of truth for cross-device persistent metrics. Pages fetch this on load. Updates flow: Raquel tells Claude in chat -> Claude commits update -> live on every device.

ideas.title: I can't function if I'm not making something
ideas.note:
Thesis: Creating isn't a hobby for her — it's regulation. When she stops making, the upset has nowhere to go and everything gets louder. The video probes the WHY: what changes between 'consuming' and 'creating,' between writing in a journal and performing for a camera.

Angles (pick one or stack):
1) Consume vs create — scrolling for hours makes everything heavier; making one mediocre video makes it lift. Why?
2) Perform vs journal — writing it down doesn't move it. She specifically needs the camera, the take, the watching it back. The performance is the load-bearing part.
3) The outlet question — what does an unprocessed feeling actually do when it has nowhere to land? Apparently it just sits on you until you put it somewhere.

Hook lines:
· I figured out why I was miserable for months. It's embarrassing.
· Apparently I have to be performing or I fall apart. Cool, normal discovery to make at 23.
· I can't function if I'm not making something. Let me explain why.
· Stuck and upset for months. Started filming again. Mood: completely different. We're going to figure out why on camera.

Why it hooks wide: 'I'm only okay when I'm making something' is a feeling a lot of people half-have but can't name — taps the consume-vs-create exhaustion without sounding like a productivity sermon.
